---
title: "Statistical Analysis Capabilities"
subtitle: "Functional Group Guide for Real World Analytics & Accelerators (RWAA)"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "On this page"
    number-sections: false
    smooth-scroll: true
    fig-align: center
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## Overview

The Statistical Analysis Capabilities group within RWAA builds and maintains the **technical infrastructure, dashboards, and analytical tools** that power real-world evidence generation across all Therapeutic Areas. The group translates complex RWD pipelines into scalable, reproducible, and user-facing products — enabling internal stakeholders to access, explore, and act on real-world insights efficiently.

---

## Core Responsibilities

- Designing and developing **interactive dashboards** for internal stakeholders across TAs and functions
- Building and maintaining **reusable analytical pipelines** and code libraries for RWD processing
- Creating **self-service tools** that allow HEOR, Medical Affairs, and Market Access teams to explore data without requiring bespoke analyses
- Automating recurring analyses and report generation to reduce turnaround time
- Managing **version control and deployment** of analytical tools across RWAA's cloud environment
- Ensuring **reproducibility and documentation standards** across all tool and dashboard outputs

---

## Key Tools & Technologies

| Area | Tools / Technologies |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard development** | R Shiny, Quarto, Power BI |
| **Programming languages** | R, Python, SQL |
| **Cloud & compute** | AWS, STRADA |
| **Version control** | Git, internal code repositories |
| **Data access & wrangling** | Spark, dbt, internal data lake connections |
| **Visualization libraries** | ggplot2, Plotly, Highcharter |

: {.striped .hover}

---

## How to Engage

Statistical Analysis Capabilities is the right team to engage when a **repeatable, scalable, or interactive** output is needed — rather than a one-time study deliverable. Common engagement triggers include recurring reporting needs, multi-TA data exploration tools, and automation of manual analytical workflows.

::: {.callout-note}
## When to Engage This Team
Reach out to Statistical Analysis Capabilities when you need a dashboard, a reusable analytical tool, or an automated pipeline — rather than a single study analysis. The earlier they are engaged in scoping, the better the final product can be designed for the end user.
:::

---

## Resources & Links

- [RWAA Study Request Process (Internal)](#)
- [Tool & Dashboard Catalog (Internal)](#)
- [STRADA / AWS Access Guide (Internal)](#)

---

*Content or technical issues with this site? [Click here](maintenance.qmd) to report any issues or provide feedback.*
